pattern: Only consecutive non-empty rows matter for beams — reducing each row to its device count and multiplying counts between successive non-empty rows (skipping fully empty rows without resetting) gives the total.
hints: Reduce each row to just the number of devices it holds — the exact positions within a row do not matter for how many beams originate from it.
Beams only form between a pair of rows that both contain at least one device, and the beam count between such a pair equals the product of their device counts.

Track the device count of the most recently seen non-empty row while scanning top to bottom; for every new non-empty row, add (previous count x current count) to the running total and update the previous count — a fully empty row is skipped entirely, leaving the tracked previous count untouched.

constraints.note: each bank[i] has length 1..500 and consists only of the characters '0' and '1'

edge_inputs.description: single row with devices — no second row to beam to
single row, no devices at all
one empty row separating two non-empty rows
two consecutive empty rows between non-empty rows — must not reset across each empty row individually
single device column repeated down many rows — chains of beams between every consecutive pair
maximum size 500x500 grid, one device per row in the same column

stress.description: random bank with random row/column counts up to the max constraint
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 500))
    n = rng.randint(1, 500)
    bank = [''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n)) for _ in range(m)]
    return [bank]
